key_points: Life processes are the basic essential functions performed by living organisms to maintain their life on earth.
Nutrition is the process of intake of nutrients and its utilization by the body for energy and growth.
Autotrophic nutrition involves organisms making their own food (like plants), while Heterotrophic nutrition involves depending on others for food.
Respiration is the process of breaking down food (glucose) to release energy in the form of ATP.
Transportation is the movement of food, water, and waste from one part of the body to another.
examples: Autotrophs: Green plants and some bacteria that perform photosynthesis.
Parasitic Nutrition: Organisms like Cuscuta (Amarbel) or leeches that derive nutrition from a host without killing it.
Saprotrophic Nutrition: Fungi like bread moulds and mushrooms that break down food outside the body.
Excretion: Removal of toxic metabolic wastes like urea from the body.
formulas: Energy Currency: $	ext{ADP} + 	ext{Phosphate} \xrightarrow{	ext{Energy}} 	ext{ATP}$
Aerobic Respiration: $	ext{Glucose} \xrightarrow{	ext{Cytoplasm}} 	ext{Pyruvate} \xrightarrow{	ext{Mitochondria}} CO_2 + H_2O + 	ext{Energy}$
exam_tips: Remember that the site of photosynthesis in a cell is the chloroplast.
Identify the 'stomata' as the tiny pores on the surface of leaves responsible for gas exchange.
Note that the main purpose of digestion is to break down complex food into simple, absorbable molecules.
Keep in mind that even when we are asleep, life processes like breathing and heartbeat continue to maintain the body.
